title: marsilea distorta
intro: Marsilea distorta is a type of aquatic plant, also known as the water clover or Indian water clover, belonging to the Marsileaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, and its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Marsilea distorta include its potential to aid in the treatment of urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and other kidney-related issues, as well as its ability to help reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used in the form of teas, infusions, or extracts, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
